Even though he was 48 points behind the championship leader Vettel after 16 rounds of the {{F1|2012}} season, Räikkönen said he took inspiration from the {{F1|2007}} season<ref name="Kimi takes inspiration from 2007">{{cite web | title=Kimi Raikkonen takes inspiration from 2007 title | website=ESPN UK | date=8 August 2020 | url=http://en.espnf1.com/lotusf1/motorsport/story/92287.html | access-date=8 August 2020 | archive-date=28 September 2013 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928005315/http://en.espnf1.com/lotusf1/motorsport/story/92287.html | url-status=live }}</ref> which he won at the last round in {{F1 GP|2007|Brazilian}} after Lewis Hamilton suffered gearbox problems.

At the {{F1 GP|2012|Indian}}, Räikkönen qualified in seventh place. He could not manage to improve his starting position because of a lack of top speed on the straights. After this race, the contract with Lotus was extended for {{F1|2013}} due to the fact that the terms of options in the contract have been met.

On 4 November 2012, Räikkönen won his first race for the Lotus F1 team at the {{F1 GP|2012|Abu Dhabi}} after a good start from fourth saw him take [[Pastor Maldonado]] and [[Mark Webber (racing driver)|Mark Webber]] at the first corner. He was unable to match the pace of Hamilton, but a mechanical issue retired the McLaren and allowed Räikkönen to win the race, despite pressure from the Ferrari of Alonso in the closing stages. Although he was now mathematically eliminated from the championship as a result of Vettel finishing third, the Lotus name celebrated its first win since the [[1987 Detroit Grand Prix]] which was won by [[Ayrton Senna]]. Still third, Räikkönen was 16 points clear of Hamilton in the championship. But after Hamilton's pole position at the [[2012 Brazilian Grand Prix|concluding race of 2012]], Räikkönen would have to finish at least fifth in the race if Hamilton were to win. He qualified only ninth. On lap 52, Räikkönen, who had been in-and-out of the points frequently, made a mistake and went off the track and tried to use an escape road to re-enter the track rather than drive over the grass. The road was blocked and he was forced to turn back. He lost numerous places and found himself behind a [[Caterham F1|Caterham]] and a [[Marussia F1|Marussia]]. He skidded again to be lapped, but re-claimed the lost places and with a retirement from Hamilton, finished the race in 10th and claimed his third place in the standings, with a single point contribution to the tally. He ended the season 71 points behind Alonso and 74 points behind champion Vettel.

====2013====
On 29 October 2012, Lotus confirmed that Räikkönen would be racing with the team in 2013, after several weeks of speculation that Räikkönen had several other options for 2013, including other forms of motorsport.

[[File:Kimi Raikkonen 2013 Malaysia FP1.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2013 Malaysian Grand Prix]]]]
On 17 March 2013, Räikkönen won the first race of the season, the {{F1 GP|2013|Australian}} despite starting from seventh, thanks to a two-stop strategy while most of the others did three stops. He also set the fastest lap of the race on lap 56. He described the victory as one of his easiest wins.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.crash.net/f1/news/188970/1/one_of_my_easiest_wins_declares_raikkonen.html|title='One of my easiest wins', declares Raikkonen|work=Crash.net|publisher=Crash Media Group|date=17 March 2013|access-date=29 March 2013|archive-date=20 March 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130320111505/http://www.crash.net/f1/news/188970/1/one_of_my_easiest_wins_declares_raikkonen.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen qualified seventh in [[2013 Malaysian Grand Prix|Malaysia]], but was demoted three places for impeding [[Nico Rosberg]] during qualifying.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.formula1.com/news/headlines/2013/3/14399.html|title=Raikkonen handed three-place grid penalty for impeding|work=Formula1.com|publisher=[[Formula One Group|Formula One Administration]]|date=23 March 2013|access-date=29 March 2013|archive-date=26 March 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130326040501/http://www.formula1.com/news/headlines/2013/3/14399.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He finished the race seventh, behind team-mate Grosjean, after damaging his car at the start.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.formula1.com/news/headlines/2013/3/14409.html|title=Malaysia: selected team and driver quotes|work=Formula1.com|publisher=[[Formula One Group|Formula One Administration]]|date=24 March 2013|access-date=29 March 2013|archive-date=21 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131121063333/http://www.formula1.com/news/headlines/2013/3/14409.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

At the {{F1 GP|2013|Chinese}}, Räikkönen qualified and finished second, despite having to regain the two places he lost at the start and having damaged the front of his car while battling [[Sergio Pérez]] for position. In the {{F1 GP|2013|Bahrain}} he qualified ninth, but was promoted to eighth after Lewis Hamilton received a five-place grid penalty for an unscheduled gearbox change. He finished the race second ahead of his team-mate [[Romain Grosjean]], with Sebastian Vettel winning the race. The podium of the race was exactly same as in [[2012 Bahrain Grand Prix|2012]]. At the {{F1 GP|2013|Spanish}}, Räikkönen started fourth and finished second thanks to a three-stop strategy. At the next race in [[2013 Monaco Grand Prix|Monaco]], Räikkönen started fifth but an aggressive overtake by Pérez gave him a puncture and dropped him out of the points until a last-ditch attempt on the final lap where he overtook [[Esteban Gutiérrez]], [[Valtteri Bottas]] and [[Nico Hülkenberg]] to gain 10th place. In the process, it continued his streak of 23 consecutive points finishes, one shy of the record of 24 set by [[Michael Schumacher]] across three seasons between 2001 and 2003.

With his Lotus struggling during the {{F1 GP|2013|Canadian}}, Räikkönen finished ninth. On 30 June 2013 at the {{F1 GP|2013|British}}, Räikkönen finished in the points for the 25th consecutive race, breaking Schumacher's record. He finished fifth but believed second place could have been possible had he pitted during the safety car period. The result kept him in third place in the championship. At the {{F1 GP|2013|German}}, Räikkönen qualified fourth and finished second, only a second behind Vettel with the help of the safety car. Räikkönen struggled in qualifying for the {{F1 GP|2013|Hungarian}}, unable to capitalise on strong times earlier in the weekend. He qualified sixth but finished second – for the fifth time at the circuit – holding off a last minute charge from Vettel with fresher tyres. The result promoted him to second in the Drivers' Championship, one point ahead of Fernando Alonso and 38 points behind championship leader, Vettel.

At the {{F1 GP|2013|Belgian}}, Räikkönen finished fastest in Q2 but struggled during the wetter final session, qualifying eighth behind his teammate. Although Räikkönen did gain some places in the race, he was eventually forced to retire with a brake related issue on lap 27, ending the Finn's record-breaking run – also numbering 27 – of consecutive points finishes. The DNF also ended his chances of beating the most consecutive race finishes – a record held by [[Nick Heidfeld]] at 41 finishes – Räikkönen recorded 38.

[[File:2013 Italian GP - Kimi.jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen at the [[2013 Italian Grand Prix]]]]
Räikkönen failed to score again at the {{F1 GP|2013|Italian}} after losing his front wing at the start. He managed to make his way up the order, but was unable to pass [[Jenson Button]] for a point scoring position. On the week following the race, it was announced that Räikkönen would not continue with Lotus for the {{F1|2014}} season, and instead join Fernando Alonso at Ferrari.<ref name=BBC-2014Ferrari>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/formula1/24042352|title=Kimi Raikkonen joins Ferrari as Fernando Alonso's team-mate|last=Benson|first=Andrew|date=10 September 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=12 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131112102439/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/formula1/24042352|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name=Telegraph-2014Ferrari /> Before the following {{F1 GP|2013|Singapore}}, it was revealed that Räikkönen had not been paid salary by Lotus for the whole season, meaning there were several million euros of outstanding fees. Räikkönen publicly cited this as the reason for leaving Lotus for Ferrari.<ref name=BBC-Salary>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/formula1/24159684|title=Kimi Raikkonen joining Ferrari because of money dispute|last=Benson|first=Andrew|date=19 September 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=8 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131008020213/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/formula1/24159684|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name=Guardian-salary />

In the {{F1 GP|2013|Singapore}}, Räikkönen had recurring back problems during the practice sessions, which at first put his participation in the race in question.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110024|title=Singapore GP: Kimi Raikkonen to qualify despite back pain issue|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|date=21 September 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=24 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130924053350/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110024|url-status=live}}</ref> He qualified only 13th, but was still able to return to the podium, clawing to the third place partly thanks to a safety car intervention in the middle of the race.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110064/|title=Singapore GP: Kimi Raikkonen says back pain no problem in race|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|author2=Beer, Matt|date=22 September 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=10 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131110115440/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110064/|url-status=live}}</ref> At the {{F1 GP|2013|Japanese}}, he qualified ninth and finished fifth.

Two weeks later, at the {{F1 GP|2013|Indian}}, Räikkönen qualified sixth. In the race, he opted for a one-stop tyre strategy, placing him in the second place at the closing stages of the Grand Prix. However, during the final few laps, his pace was more than a second slower than those chasing him, including Nico Rosberg and Räikkönen's Lotus teammate Romain Grosjean. With eight laps remaining, Rosberg was able to take over Räikkönen for second. Grosjean then caught up with Räikkönen on the fourth last lap, but was not immediately able to pass him.<ref name=2013-India>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110921|title=Indian GP: Vettel takes fourth F1 title with crushing win|last=O'Leary|first=Jamie|date=27 October 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=30 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131030041256/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110921|url-status=live}}</ref> At this point, the Lotus trackside operations director Alan Permane had a heated radio exchange with Räikkönen, commanding him to move out of the way. The radio conversation was widely noted in the press, raising questions about the health of his relationship with the team.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110961|title=Eric Boullier apologises for Kimi Raikkonen/Lotus F1 radio exchange|last=Elizalde|first=Pablo|date=28 October 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=31 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131031144944/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/110961|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.f1times.co.uk/news/display/08164|title=Lotus apologise for radio outburst at Raikkonen|date=29 October 2013|work=The F1 Times|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=1 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131201192445/http://www.f1times.co.uk/news/display/08164|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen eventually finished seventh after stopping for new tyres with only two laps to go.<ref name=2013-India />

In the following {{F1 GP|2013|Abu Dhabi}}, questions about Räikkönen's relationship with his team were further fuelled by his absence from the usual Thursday media events. Räikkönen had reportedly only left his home at the last moment to make it to the Grand Prix after considering not racing at all in the event.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111000|title=Kimi Raikkonen absence fuels doubts over Lotus F1 team relationship|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|date=31 October 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=3 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131103144522/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111000|url-status=live}}</ref> On Friday, Räikkönen then publicly threatened that he would not continue to race with Lotus in the remaining two Grands Prix of the season after Abu Dhabi unless the salary dispute was resolved.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/formula1/24776816|title=Kimi Raikkonen threatens to boycott races over pay dispute|last=Benson|first=Andrew|date=1 November 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=13 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131113221433/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/formula1/24776816|url-status=live}}</ref> In the Saturday's qualifying, Räikkönen made it to fifth, but was demoted to the 22nd and last grid spot due to his E21 car failing a post-qualifying floor deflection test.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111084|title=Abu Dhabi GP: Kimi Raikkonen's Lotus excluded from qualifying|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|date=2 November 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=6 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131106092402/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111084|url-status=live}}</ref> In the race, he immediately made contact with [[Giedo van der Garde]]'s [[Caterham F1|Caterham]] in the first corner of the first lap and broke his Lotus's right front suspension, forcing Räikkönen to retire on the first lap for the first time since the [[2006 United States Grand Prix]]. Immediately after being recovered, he left the circuit and returned to his hotel while the race continued, further fuelling tensions between him and the team. Despite this, following the race, it was announced that Lotus and Räikkönen had reached a provisional agreement on the salary dispute that would see Räikkönen race for the team during the remainder of the season<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111095|title=Kimi Raikkonen to see out 2013 F1 season with Lotus as row settled|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|date=3 November 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=6 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131106073542/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111095|url-status=live}}</ref> as the investment group Quantum Motorsports said that they had concluded long-running negotiations with Lotus for acquisition of a share in the team, providing the team with financial security.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111124|title=Quantum says its Lotus Formula 1 investment deal is done|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|date=3 November 2013|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=6 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131106092407/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111124|url-status=live}}</ref>

However, a week before the following {{F1 GP|2013|United States}}, it was announced that Räikkönen would nevertheless miss the rest of the season, having elected to have back surgery for the problems that had troubled him in the Singapore Grand Prix.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111218|first=Jonathan|last=Noble|work=[[Autosport]]|publisher=[[Haymarket Media Group|Haymarket Publications]]|title=Kimi Raikkonen to miss rest of 2013 F1 season for back surgery|date=10 November 2013|access-date=10 November 2013|archive-date=10 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131110103545/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/111218|url-status=live}}</ref> For the remaining two Grands Prix, Räikkönen would be replaced at Lotus by fellow Finn [[Heikki Kovalainen]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/formula-one/24946180|title=Heikki Kovalainen replaces Kimi Raikkonen at Lotus|date=14 November 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=18 November 2013|archive-date=18 November 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131118035609/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/formula-one/24946180|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Return to Ferrari (2014–2018)===
[[File:Kimi Raikkonen 2014 Singapore FP1.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2014 Singapore Grand Prix]]]]
On 11 September 2013, it was confirmed that Räikkönen had agreed a two-year deal to return to Ferrari (where he won the championship in {{F1|2007}}), starting from {{F1|2014}}.<ref name=BBC-2014Ferrari /><ref name=Telegraph-2014Ferrari>{{cite web|title=Kimi Raikkonen signs two-year deal to partner Fernando Alonso at Ferrari following Felipe Massa departure|date=11 September 2013 |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/10301987/Kimi-Raikkonen-signs-two-year-deal-to-partner-Fernando-Alonso-at-Ferrari-following-Felipe-Massa-departure.html|publisher=Telegraph|access-date=8 October 2013|archive-date=14 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130914015637/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/10301987/Kimi-Raikkonen-signs-two-year-deal-to-partner-Fernando-Alonso-at-Ferrari-following-Felipe-Massa-departure.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He revealed that it was for monetary reasons that he left Lotus.<ref name=BBC-Salary /><ref name=Guardian-salary>{{cite web|title=Kimi Raikkonen: I left Lotus for Ferrari in dispute over money|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/sep/19/kimi-raikkonen-money-lotus-ferrari|work=The Guardian|date=19 September 2013|access-date=8 October 2013|archive-date=19 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130919231625/http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/sep/19/kimi-raikkonen-money-lotus-ferrari|url-status=live}}</ref>

====2014====
Räikkönen was on for a podium finish at the [[2014 Monaco Grand Prix|Monaco Grand Prix]]. However, [[Max Chilton]] punctured Räikkönen's left-rear tyre while unlapping himself, forcing Räikkönen to make a second pitstop.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/114139/raikkonen-chilton-destroyed-race|title=Monaco GP: Kimi Raikkonen says Max Chilton destroyed his race|last=Anderson|first=Ben|website=Autosport.com|access-date=7 December 2019|archive-date=7 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191207122944/https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/114139/raikkonen-chilton-destroyed-race|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen then set the fastest lap of the race on lap 75, but finished 12th.

After a mostly disappointing first half of the season, Räikkönen had a return to form at the first race back after the summer break in [[2014 Belgian Grand Prix|Belgium]], where a good strategy and a series of fastest laps earned him fourth place, his best result of the season. It was also the first time Räikkönen had finished higher in a race than Alonso in 2014. Räikkönen ended the 2014 season a career-low 12th in the Drivers' Championship, and for the first time since his rookie year, did not finish on the podium.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Smith|first1=Luke|last2=DiZinno|first2=Tony|url=http://motorsportstalk.nbcsports.com/2014/11/30/f1-2014-driver-review-kimi-raikkonen/|title=F1 2014 Driver Review: Kimi Raikkonen|publisher=NBC Sports|date=30 November 2014|access-date=9 January 2015|archive-date=2 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150102121232/http://motorsportstalk.nbcsports.com/2014/11/30/f1-2014-driver-review-kimi-raikkonen/|url-status=live}}</ref> Throughout the season, Räikkönen struggled with the car's lack of turn-in on corner entry and the feeling of the new brake-by-wire systems, saying that "We try somehow to balance it out and try to have a front end on the car, but it is very, very difficult. I hate it when there is no front end on the car."<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/114639/raikkonen-hurt-by-perfect-storm|title=Kimi Raikkonen hurt by perfect storm during 2014 F1 season|last=Noble|first=Jonathan|website=Autosport.com|date=26 June 2014 |access-date=7 December 2019|archive-date=7 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191207122944/https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/114639/raikkonen-hurt-by-perfect-storm|url-status=live}}</ref>

Räikkönen remained with Ferrari for the 2015 season, partnering former world champion Sebastian Vettel after Alonso announced his departure from the Scuderia.

====2015====
[[File:Kimi_Raikkonen_2015_Malaysia_FP2.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2015 Malaysian Grand Prix]]]]
Räikkönen had to retire from the first race of the season in [[2015 Australian Grand Prix|Australia]] due to a loose wheel following a pitstop.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/australian-grand-prix-report-hamilton-leads-all-the-way-but-insists-its-not-a-procession-10108887.html|first=David|last=Tremayne|title=Australian Grand Prix report: Hamilton leads all the way, but insists it's not a procession|work=The Independent|date=15 March 2015|access-date=12 April 2015|author-link=David Tremayne|archive-date=12 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180212204606/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/australian-grand-prix-report-hamilton-leads-all-the-way-but-insists-its-not-a-procession-10108887.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Following that mishap, he recovered to finish fourth both in [[2015 Malaysian Grand Prix|Malaysia]] and [[2015 Chinese Grand Prix|China]]. Another pit issue befell Räikkönen during practice for the {{F1 GP|2015|Bahrain}} as he was reprimanded for exiting the pitlane in a 'potentially dangerous manner' by the stewards, having swerved around a standing car in an undesignated area. However, he escaped a possible grid penalty.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/9809973/kimi-raikkonen-reprimanded-for-driving-in-a-potentially-dangerous-manner|title=Kimi Raikkonen reprimanded for driving in a 'potentially dangerous manner'|publisher=Sky Sports|date=17 April 2015|access-date=17 April 2015|archive-date=18 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150418004612/http://www1.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/9809973/kimi-raikkonen-reprimanded-for-driving-in-a-potentially-dangerous-manner|url-status=live}}</ref> During the same weekend, his manager [[Steve Robertson (racing driver)|Steve Robertson]] suggested that Räikkönen was heading towards a contract extension with Ferrari.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://adamcooperf1.com/2015/04/17/raikkonen-heading-for-extended-ferrari-deal-says-robertson/|title=Raikkonen heading for extended Ferrari deal, says Robertson|publisher=Adam Cooper F1|date=17 April 2015|access-date=17 April 2015|first=Adam|last=Cooper|archive-date=19 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150419021555/http://adamcooperf1.com/2015/04/17/raikkonen-heading-for-extended-ferrari-deal-says-robertson/|url-status=live}}</ref> That would contradict previous suggestions from Räikkönen himself that he would 'probably' retire at the end of his Ferrari contract, and that 2015 would be his last season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/114785|first=Jonathan|last=Noble|title=Kimi Raikkonen likely to leave F1 when Ferrari deal ends after 2015|date=4 July 2014|access-date=17 April 2015|work=[[Autosport]]|publisher=[[Haymarket Media Group|Haymarket Publications]]|archive-date=18 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150418012758/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/114785|url-status=live}}</ref> Courtesy of a considerate tyre strategy by the Ferrari team, at the {{F1 GP|2015|Bahrain}}, Räikkönen finished second after gaining that position late in the race and starting from fourth on the grid.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/f1/news/12514/9813887|first=James|last=Galloway|title=Bahrain GP: Lewis Hamilton claims another win ahead of duelling rivals|publisher=Sky Sports|date=19 April 2015|access-date=19 April 2015|archive-date=19 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150419202251/http://www1.skysports.com/f1/news/12514/9813887|url-status=live}}</ref> In the process, Räikkönen recorded his first podium since the [[2013 Korean Grand Prix]] for Lotus, and the first in his second spell with Ferrari – his most recent Ferrari podium was a third-place finish at the [[2009 Italian Grand Prix]]. Räikkönen said that he was hopeful in winning races in the near future after the team's strong recovery relative to {{F1|2014}}. He also commented that, both the engine and downforce had been significantly improved and that the car handled much more to his liking.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/118603|first=Lawrence|last=Barretto|title=F1 Bahrain: Ferrari's Kimi Raikkonen promises much more to come|date=19 April 2015|access-date=20 April 2015|work=[[Autosport]]|publisher=[[Haymarket Media Group|Haymarket Publications]]|archive-date=20 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150420045031/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/118603|url-status=live}}</ref> In a post-race interview, Ferrari team boss, [[Maurizio Arrivabene]], described this result as a sign that Räikkönen "is back" and that he "showed what a race animal he is", also implying that if he had a few more race laps available, Räikkönen would have challenged Hamilton for the race win.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.planetf1.com/driver/3213/44366/Raikkonen-showed-hes-a-race-animal |title='Raikkonen showed he's a race animal' |publisher=PlanetF1.com |date=20 April 2015 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150422170152/http://www.planetf1.com/driver/3213/44366/Raikkonen-showed-hes-a-race-animal |archive-date=22 April 2015}}</ref>

At the next race of the season in [[2015 Spanish Grand Prix|Barcelona]], Räikkönen was unhappy with the setup of the car and was able to qualify only in seventh place. However, he had a good opening lap in the race and made up two places to finish the race in fifth position.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/formula1/32458340 |title=How the Spanish GP unfolded |date=10 May 2015 |publisher=BBC |access-date=26 May 2020 |archive-date=25 May 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210525044359/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/formula1/32458340 |url-status=live }}</ref> Räikkönen termed [[2015 Monaco Grand Prix|Monaco GP]] qualifying session to be a "disaster"; he was only able to qualify in sixth position, with his teammate Vettel qualifying in third, followed by [[Daniel Ricciardo]] and [[Daniil Kvyat]] in the Red Bulls.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thisisf1.com/2015/05/23/raikkonen-frustrated-with-disaster-qualifying-in-monaco-gp/|title='Raikkonen frustrated with "disaster" qualifying in Monaco GP'|publisher=f1fanatic.co.uk|date=23 May 2015|access-date=22 September 2015|archive-date=13 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180213021615/http://www.thisisf1.com/2015/05/23/raikkonen-frustrated-with-disaster-qualifying-in-monaco-gp/|url-status=dead}}</ref> In the race, Räikkönen was able to move up to fifth place, before being controversially overtaken by [[Daniel Ricciardo|Ricciardo]], and thus held his qualifying position to finish the race in sixth. Räikkönen put up a strong qualifying performance at [[2015 Canadian Grand Prix|Montreal]] to take third on the grid behind the two Mercedes drivers. During the race, while on the out-lap from a pitstop, Räikkönen suffered a repeat of the incident at the previous year's race and spun at the hairpin, which was attributed to an engine torque mapping issue. This caused him to lose his third place to [[Valtteri Bottas]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/11657741/Canadian-Grand-Prix-2015-live.html|title='Canadian Grand Prix 2015: Lewis Hamilton cruises to victory'|last=Hodges|first=Vicki|publisher=telegraph.co.uk|date=7 June 2015|access-date=4 April 2018|archive-date=1 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181101015734/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/11657741/Canadian-Grand-Prix-2015-live.html|url-status=live}}</ref> In the [[2015 Austrian Grand Prix|Austrian Grand Prix]] he was involved in a big crash with Fernando Alonso which left Alonso's McLaren on top of Räikkönen's Ferrari. However both left unscathed. After a disappointing showing caused by changing weather in Great Britain, Räikkönen bounced back strongly running second behind Vettel looking set for a 1–2 in [[2015 Hungarian Grand Prix|Hungary]], when a technical failure regarding the energy recovery system meant he lost straightline speed. An untimely safety car meant losing further positions after the restart and after a pitstop to re-fire the engine meant he dropped out of the points he retired when the problem did not fix itself as he had lost the chance to get back past any cars.

On 19 August, Ferrari announced that Räikkönen had extended his contract with the team for the 2016 season, taking his total tally with the team to six seasons spread out over a total of ten years.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://formula1.ferrari.com/en/scuderia-ferrari-raikkonens-contract-extended/|title=Raikkonen's contract extended|publisher=Scuderia Ferrari|date=19 August 2015|access-date=19 August 2015|archive-date=21 August 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150821022234/http://formula1.ferrari.com/en/scuderia-ferrari-raikkonens-contract-extended/|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen said that 'his dream went on' and confirmed that he wanted Ferrari to be the team where he ended his career.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://formula1.ferrari.com/en/raikkonen-the-dream-goes-on/|title=Raikkonen: The dream goes on|publisher=Scuderia Ferrari|date=19 August 2015|access-date=19 August 2015}}</ref> At the race following the announcement ([[2015 Belgian Grand Prix|Spa]]), miscommunication from the team coupled with gearbox change penalties saw Räikkönen start from 17th on the grid. However, he managed to overtake the middle pack and finished the race in seventh, after Vettel had a tire blowout on the 42nd lap causing him to lose a third-place finish.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/11819190/Belgian-Grand-Prix-2015-Lewis-Hamilton-strolls-to-victory-at-Spa.html|title='Belgian Grand Prix 2015: Lewis Hamilton strolls to victory at Spa'|last=Johnson|first=Daniel|publisher=telegraph.co.uk|date=23 August 2015|access-date=4 April 2018|archive-date=1 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181101015616/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/motorsport/formulaone/11819190/Belgian-Grand-Prix-2015-Lewis-Hamilton-strolls-to-victory-at-Spa.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[File:Kimi Räikkönen qualifies second (21463936202).jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen at the [[2015 Italian Grand Prix]]]]Räikkönen qualified on the front row for Ferrari's home race at [[2015 Italian Grand Prix|Monza]], but a complete lack of movement for a few seconds at the start saw him drop down the order. Showing good pace however, he managed to pick his way from the back of the field to fifth. Räikkönen blamed a 'clutch positioning' problem, while team boss Arrivabene suggested Räikkönen had caused the [[Anti-stall (engine)|anti-stall]] to kick in himself due to 'messing with his fingers' although he clarified it saying he did not fully know at the time.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/120692|last=Barretto|first=Lawrence|title=Kimi Raikkonen puts bad start down to Ferrari F1 clutch|publisher=Autosport|date=6 September 2015|access-date=11 September 2015|archive-date=8 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150908203213/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/120692|url-status=live}}</ref> Nevertheless, he remained at one podium of the season, as teammate Vettel finished second to take his tally to eight in twelve races.

[[File:Räikkönen Singapore GP 2015.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2015 Singapore Grand Prix]]]]
At the [[2015 Singapore Grand Prix|Singapore Grand Prix]], Räikkönen showed consistent performances in the practice sessions and qualified in third, behind Vettel and [[Daniel Ricciardo]]. Räikkönen maintained the position through the race despite being uncomfortable with the car, to take his second podium finish of the season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.espn.co.uk/f1/story/_/id/13700007/kimi-raikkonen-third-not-ideal-take-it|title=Kimi Raikkonen on third: 'Not ideal – but I'll take it'|last=Saunders|first=Nate|publisher=espn.co.uk|date=20 September 2015|access-date=26 May 2020|archive-date=19 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190819153100/https://www.espn.co.uk/f1/story/_/id/13700007/kimi-raikkonen-third-not-ideal-take-it|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen ended the season by finishing third in the season-ending [[2015 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix|Abu Dhabi Grand Prix]] to take his third podium of the year and securing fourth place in the Drivers' Championship.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.formula1.com/content/fom-website/en/championship/results/2015-race-results/2015-abu-dhabi-results/race.html|title=2015 Formula 1 Etihad Airways Abu Dhabi Grand Prix – Race|work=Formula1.com|access-date=1 February 2016|archive-date=27 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160127101445/http://www.formula1.com/content/fom-website/en/championship/results/2015-race-results/2015-abu-dhabi-results/race.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

====2016====

====2017====
[[File:２時間待つとか思ってませんでした (36974717342).jpg|thumb|right|Räikkönen during qualifying at the [[2017 Italian Grand Prix]]. His [[Ferrari SF70H]] is using the special 70th Scuderia Ferrari anniversary livery.]]
Räikkönen started the 2017 season with fourth in [[2017 Australian Grand Prix|Australia]], fifth in [[2017 Chinese Grand Prix|China]] and then again fourth in [[2017 Bahrain Grand Prix|Bahrain]]. Räikkönen scored his first podium of the season at the following [[2017 Russian Grand Prix|Russian Grand Prix]], finishing third. He retired from the [[2017 Spanish Grand Prix|Spanish Grand Prix]] after being involved in an incident on the first lap.

Räikkönen scored his first pole position in 129 races at the {{F1 GP|2017|Monaco}}, qualifying 0.04 seconds faster than teammate Sebastian Vettel to become the sport's oldest polesitter since ====2018====
[[File:Kimi Raikkonen 2018 Azerbaijan GP.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Kimi Räikkönen at the [[2018 Azerbaijan Grand Prix]]]]
Räikkönen renewed his contract with Ferrari for the {{F1|2018}} season on 22 August 2017.<ref>{{cite web|title=Renewal of the agreement between Scuderia Ferrari and Kimi Raikkonen|url=http://formula1.ferrari.com/en/2017-f1-renewal-of-the-agreement-between-scuderia-ferrari-and-kimi-raikkonen/|website=formula1.ferrari.com|access-date=22 August 2017|date=22 August 2017|archive-date=22 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170822180439/http://formula1.ferrari.com/en/2017-f1-renewal-of-the-agreement-between-scuderia-ferrari-and-kimi-raikkonen/|url-status=live}}</ref> He qualified second for the first race in [[2018 Australian Grand Prix|Australia]] and finished third, after being jumped by Vettel during the virtual safety car period. At the [[2018 Bahrain Grand Prix|Bahrain Grand Prix]], he qualified second after encountering traffic on his last run.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.what-the-teams-said-qualifying-in-sakhir.42X0CqVi7maawiQEKiQSMy.html|title=What the teams said – Qualifying in Sakhir|website=Formula1.com|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=3 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803091732/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.what-the-teams-said-qualifying-in-sakhir.42X0CqVi7maawiQEKiQSMy.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He then ran in third for most of the race, but retired on lap 35 due to a pit stop error by Ferrari.<ref>{{cite web|title=Bahrain GP: Ferrari mechanic breaks leg in Kimi Raikkonen pit lane accident|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/formula1/43693181|website=BBC Sport|access-date=11 April 2018|date=9 April 2018|archive-date=11 April 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180411063758/http://www.bbc.com/sport/formula1/43693181|url-status=live}}</ref> He went on to finish third at the [[2018 Chinese Grand Prix|Chinese Grand Prix]] after recovering from a strategy aimed at helping his teammate.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.racefans.net/2018/04/17/how-ferrari-vettel-first-strategy-ruined-raikkonen-race/|title=Analysis: How Ferrari's Vettel-first strategy ruined Raikkonen's race|last=Collantine|first=Keith|website=racefans.net|date=17 April 2018|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=14 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230414023642/https://www.racefans.net/2018/04/17/how-ferrari-vettel-first-strategy-ruined-raikkonen-race/|url-status=live}}</ref>

At the [[2018 Azerbaijan Grand Prix|Azerbaijan Grand Prix]], Räikkönen recovered from a first lap incident with [[Esteban Ocon]] to eventually finish second. Räikkönen's engine failed during practice at the [[2018 Spanish Grand Prix|Spanish Grand Prix]], forcing him to change engines.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-engine-change-spanish-gp-1036193/3083873/?v=2&s=1&q=Raikkonen|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220602120652/https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-engine-change-spanish-gp-1036193/3083873/?v=2&s=1&q=Raikkonen|url-status=dead|archive-date=2 June 2022|title=Ferrari to replace Raikkonen's engine after FP2 issue|website=www.motorsport.com|access-date=4 April 2020}}</ref> This meant that he missed Ferrari's upgraded engine introduced two races later in [[2018 Canadian Grand Prix|Canada]] and had to race with the old specification engine until the 13th round in [[2018 Belgian Grand Prix|Belgium]] to avoid a penalty.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.planetf1.com/news/mercedes-ferrari-bring-engine-upgrades-to-spa/|title=Mercedes, Ferrari unveil Spec-3 engines|website=PlanetF1|date=24 August 2018|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=1 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001201224/https://www.planetf1.com/news/mercedes-ferrari-bring-engine-upgrades-to-spa/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.thursdays-hot-topic-which-pu-manufacturer-will-deliver-the-best-upgrade.56w600T9wscoWIWy4yecKe.html|title=Thursday's Hot Topic – Which PU manufacturer will deliver the best upgrade?|website=Formula1.com|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=19 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200919095844/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.thursdays-hot-topic-which-pu-manufacturer-will-deliver-the-best-upgrade.56w600T9wscoWIWy4yecKe.html|url-status=live}}</ref> His engine failed again in the race in Spain, forcing him to retire from the race, but the engine did not have to be replaced.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-no-engine-change-spain-failure-1037736/3091333/|title=Raikkonen engine failure unlikely to prompt another change|website=www.motorsport.com|date=15 May 2018 |access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=7 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707090600/https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-no-engine-change-spain-failure-1037736/3091333/|url-status=live}}</ref> This was followed by a fourth-place finish in [[2018 Monaco Grand Prix|Monaco]] and sixth in Canada.
[[File:FIA F1 Austria 2018 Nr. 7 Räikkönen.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2018 Austrian Grand Prix]]]]
A third-place finish in the [[2018 French Grand Prix|French Grand Prix]] started a run of consecutive podiums for Räikkönen. He would finish second at the following [[2018 Austrian Grand Prix|Austrian Grand Prix]], then at the [[2018 British Grand Prix|British Grand Prix]] he recovered from a controversial 10-second penalty to finish third,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11430583/british-gp-why-was-kimi-raikkonen-penalty-10-not-five-seconds|title=British GP: Why was Kimi Raikkonen penalty 10 not five seconds?|website=Sky Sports|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=13 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190713153558/https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11430583/british-gp-why-was-kimi-raikkonen-penalty-10-not-five-seconds|url-status=live}}</ref> after which he finished third again at the [[2018 German Grand Prix|German]] and [[2018 Hungarian Grand Prix|Hungarian]] Grands Prix to score five consecutive podiums. At the [[2018 Belgian Grand Prix|Belgian Grand Prix]], Räikkönen had strong pace during qualifying, but a fuel miscalculation by Ferrari meant that he could not complete a lap in the drying conditions later in session which left him sixth on the grid.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-fuel-miscalculation-belgian-gp-qualiying/3164756/|title=Fuel miscalculation wrecked Raikkonen's Spa qualifying|website=www.motorsport.com|date=25 August 2018 |access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=9 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201109032548/https://www.motorsport.com/f1/news/raikkonen-fuel-miscalculation-belgian-gp-qualiying/3164756/|url-status=live}}</ref> He retired from the race as his car was damaged in a first lap crash initiated by [[Nico Hülkenberg]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://edition.cnn.com/2018/08/26/motorsport/formula-one-belgian-gp-crash-spa-spt-intl/index.html|title=Sebastian Vettel wins Belgian GP after avoiding huge opening corner crash|website=CNN|date=26 August 2018 |access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=24 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201024211816/https://edition.cnn.com/2018/08/26/motorsport/formula-one-belgian-gp-crash-spa-spt-intl/index.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

Räikkönen took pole position at the [[2018 Italian Grand Prix|Italian Grand Prix]], breaking [[Juan Pablo Montoya]]'s 14-year-old record to set the fastest lap ever recorded in Formula One history with an average speed of 263.588 kilometres per hour (163.786 mph) and a laptime of 1:19.119.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.move-over-montoya-raikkonen-breaks-record-for-fastest-lap-in-f1-history.1OyGclYbnOYIuKKEMwyQk8.html|title=MOVE OVER MONTOYA: Raikkonen breaks record for fastest lap in F1 history|website=Formula1.com|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=29 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181029152433/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.move-over-montoya-raikkonen-breaks-record-for-fastest-lap-in-f1-history.1OyGclYbnOYIuKKEMwyQk8.html|url-status=live}}</ref> His race was compromised by Mercedes ordering Bottas to block him after his pit stop,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.racefans.net/2018/09/04/bottas-helping-hamilton-didnt-compromise-race/|title=Bottas says helping Hamilton didn't compromise his result|last=Collantine|first=Keith|website=racefans.net|date=4 September 2018|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=9 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201109024332/https://www.racefans.net/2018/09/04/bottas-helping-hamilton-didnt-compromise-race/|url-status=live}}</ref> but he nevertheless finished second to score his 100th podium finish, becoming only the fifth driver ever to do so.<ref name=":0">{{cite web|url=https://badgergp.com/f1s-100-podium-club/|title=F1's 100 Podium Club › Badger GP|last=Haldenby|first=Nicky|date=3 September 2018|website=badgergp.com|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=1 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001041135/https://badgergp.com/f1s-100-podium-club/|url-status=live}}</ref> This was followed by a fifth-place finish in [[2018 Singapore Grand Prix|Singapore]], fourth in [[2018 Russian Grand Prix|Russia]] and fifth in [[2018 Japanese Grand Prix|Japan]], where his car sustained damage from a hit by Verstappen.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.crash.net/f1/news/907423/1/raikkonen-japanese-gp-fight-ended-damage-verstappen-hit|title=Raikkonen: Japanese GP fight ended by damage in Verstappen hit|date=7 October 2018|website=Crash|access-date=4 April 2020|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007153057/https://www.crash.net/f1/news/907423/1/raikkonen-japanese-gp-fight-ended-damage-verstappen-hit|url-status=live}}</ref>

Räikkönen won the [[2018 United States Grand Prix|United States Grand Prix]], becoming, at 39, F1's oldest race winner since [[Nigel Mansell]] in 1994, and the 13th oldest F1 race winner ever. This victory made Räikkönen the most successful Finnish driver of all time in terms of race wins. He broke the record for the longest gap between Grand Prix wins (113 races) and the record for the biggest gap between first and last career wins (5,691 days). Coincidentally, this victory came on the 11th anniversary of his 2007 F1 world championship.<ref name="auto">{{Cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11527364/kimi-makes-history-the-numbers|title=The numbers behind Kimi Raikkonen's record-breaking victory|date=21 October 2018|work=Sky Sports|access-date=21 October 2018|archive-date=22 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181022033632/https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11527364/kimi-makes-history-the-numbers|url-status=live}}</ref> He followed this victory with third-placed podiums at the [[2018 Mexican Grand Prix|Mexican]] and [[2018 Brazilian Grand Prix|Brazilian]] Grands Prix. He retired in [[2018 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix|Abu Dhabi]] with an electrical issue, but nonetheless, he finished third in the Drivers' Championship in his final season with Ferrari.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://formula1.ferrari.com/en/2018-f1-abu-dhabi-gp-race/|title=Abu Dhabi Grand Prix – Seb second and Kimi still third|date=28 November 2018|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181128034847/https://formula1.ferrari.com/en/2018-f1-abu-dhabi-gp-race/|archive-date=28 November 2018|access-date=4 April 2020}}</ref>

===Alfa Romeo (2019–2021)===
====2019====
[[File:Kimi_Raikkonen,_2019_Chinese_GP.jpg|thumb|right|Räikkönen at the [[2019 Chinese Grand Prix]]]]
On 11 September 2018, it was announced that Räikkönen would leave Ferrari at the end of the season to rejoin [[Sauber]] on a two-year contract, the team he made his Formula One debut with in {{F1|2001}}.<ref>{{cite web |title=Kimi Räikkönen to race for the Alfa Romeo Sauber F1 Team from 2019 |url=https://www.sauberf1team.com/news/kimi-raikkonen-to-race-for-the-alfa-romeo-sauber-f1-team-from-2019-2 |website=www.sauberf1team.com |access-date=11 September 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180911113413/https://www.sauberf1team.com/news/kimi-raikkonen-to-race-for-the-alfa-romeo-sauber-f1-team-from-2019-2 |archive-date=11 September 2018 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Räikkönen made his Sauber return in the postseason [[Yas Marina Circuit|Abu Dhabi]] Pirelli tyre test driving their [[Sauber C37|2018 car]].<ref>{{cite news |title=F1 Testing: Kimi Raikkonen makes Sauber return as Sebastian Vettel sets pace |url=https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11564902/f1-testing-kimi-raikkonen-makes-sauber-return-as-sebastian-vettel-sets-pace |access-date=28 November 2018 |work=Sky Sports F1 |date=27 November 2018 |archive-date=28 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181128210759/https://www.skysports.com/f1/news/12433/11564902/f1-testing-kimi-raikkonen-makes-sauber-return-as-sebastian-vettel-sets-pace |url-status=live }}</ref> The Sauber team was renamed [[Alfa Romeo Racing]] prior to the start of the {{F1|2019}} season.

Räikkönen had an impressive first half of the season, scoring 31 points before the summer break, placing him eighth in the Drivers' Championship after 12 races.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.statsf1.com/en/2019/hongrie/championnat.aspx|title=Hungary 2019 – Championship • STATS F1|website=www.statsf1.com|access-date=7 December 2019|archive-date=4 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190804161641/https://www.statsf1.com/en/2019/hongrie/championnat.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref> In comparison, his teammate [[Antonio Giovinazzi]] had scored only a single point during the same period. However, after the summer break, the [[Alfa Romeo Racing C38]] struggled for pace and Räikkönen endured a seven-race pointless streak. He had an opportunity to score points in [[2019 Belgian Grand Prix|Belgium]], starting from sixth on the grid, but he was hit by [[Max Verstappen]] at the first corner.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/146624/alfa-romeo-has-no-need-to-panic-over-race-pace|title=Vasseur: Alfa Romeo has no need to panic over its F1 race pace|last=Cooper|first=Adam|website=Autosport.com|date=16 October 2019 |access-date=7 December 2019|archive-date=7 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191207155141/https://www.autosport.com/f1/news/146624/alfa-romeo-has-no-need-to-panic-over-race-pace|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.verstappen-and-raikkonen-bemoan-first-lap-clash.4LEpXoR5Ha6QXl9xd2Ovty.html|title=Verstappen and Raikkonen bemoan first-lap clash|website=Formula1.com|access-date=7 December 2019|archive-date=3 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190903173008/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.verstappen-and-raikkonen-bemoan-first-lap-clash.4LEpXoR5Ha6QXl9xd2Ovty.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The pointless streak ended at the {{F1 GP|2019|Brazilian}}, where a strong race and a penalty for Lewis Hamilton yielded a fourth-place finish, the first top-four finish for the [[Hinwil]]-based team since {{F1|2013}}. Räikkönen finished 12th in the Drivers' Championship with 43 points, which, again, was the best result for a driver of the Hinwil-based team since 2013.

====2020====
[[File:Kimi Raïkkonen-Alfa Romeo C39 (1).jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen at the [[2020 Formula One pre-season testing|pre-season testing]] in Barcelona]]
Räikkönen remained at the Alfa Romeo Racing team for the [[2020 Formula One World Championship|2020 season]]. The team's Ferrari-powered car proved to be one of the slowest cars of the season,<ref>{{cite web|date=24 July 2020|title=How much is the Ferrari engine holding Haas and Alfa back?|url=https://the-race.com/formula-1/how-much-is-ferrari-engine-holding-haas-and-alfa-back/|access-date=17 September 2020|website=The Race|archive-date=1 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200901052248/https://the-race.com/formula-1/how-much-is-ferrari-engine-holding-haas-and-alfa-back/|url-status=live}}</ref> and the team was left fighting at the back of the grid against [[Haas F1 Team|Haas]] and [[Williams Grand Prix Engineering|Williams]].<ref>{{cite web|date=24 August 2020|title=The F1 'championship' Raikkonen is leading|url=https://the-race.com/formula-1/the-f1-championship-raikkonen-is-leading/|access-date=17 September 2020|website=The Race|archive-date=15 April 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210415142040/https://the-race.com/formula-1/the-f1-championship-raikkonen-is-leading/|url-status=live}}</ref> During the season Räikkönen scored points twice, finishing ninth at the [[2020 Tuscan Grand Prix|Tuscan Grand Prix]] and again ninth at the [[2020 Emilia Romagna Grand Prix|Emilia Romagna Grand Prix]], while he scored the team's best qualifying result with eighth in [[2020 Turkish Grand Prix|Turkey]]. During the [[2020 Italian Grand Prix|Italian Grand Prix]] at Monza, Räikkönen ran as high as second after a red flag and penalties to [[Lewis Hamilton]] and Giovinazzi, however ultimately finished 11th and outside the points. These results placed him 16th in the drivers' standings with four points, ahead of teammate Antonio Giovinazzi and the Haas and Williams drivers.<ref>{{cite web|title=2020 Driver Standings|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/results.html/2020/drivers.html|access-date=2 January 2021|website=Formula1.com|archive-date=28 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210128035850/https://www.formula1.com/en/results.html/2020/drivers.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen was the highest finishing driver among this group in nine of the season's 17 races,<ref>{{cite web|date=17 November 2020|title=The 'wins' that made Raikkonen the backmarker king|url=https://the-race.com/formula-1/the-wins-that-made-raikkonen-the-backmarker-king/|access-date=2 January 2021|website=The Race|archive-date=17 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201117145957/https://the-race.com/formula-1/the-wins-that-made-raikkonen-the-backmarker-king/|url-status=live}}</ref> while he finished ahead of his teammate in nine of the 13 races which both finished and qualified ahead of him eight times.<ref>{{cite web|date=15 December 2020|title=Team mate battles 2020: The final score – Raikkonen vs Giovinazzi · RaceFans|url=https://www.racefans.net/2020/12/15/team-mate-battles-2020-the-final-score-raikkonen-vs-giovinazzi/|access-date=2 January 2021|website=RaceFans|archive-date=16 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201216105312/https://www.racefans.net/2020/12/15/team-mate-battles-2020-the-final-score-raikkonen-vs-giovinazzi/|url-status=live}}</ref> He was also the highest finishing Ferrari-powered driver on four occasions.<ref>{{cite web|last=GPfans.com|title=F1 2020: Half-term driver rankings, part one|url=https://www.gpfans.com/en/articles/56966/f1-2020-half-term-driver-rankings-part-one/|access-date=2 January 2021|website=GPfans|date=17 September 2020 |archive-date=6 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201006235511/https://www.gpfans.com/en/articles/56966/f1-2020-half-term-driver-rankings-part-one/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=13 December 2020|title=Verstappen wins processional Abu Dhabi Grand Prix|url=https://formulaspy.com/f1/verstappen-wins-processional-abu-dhabi-grand-prix-73011|access-date=2 January 2021|website=FormulaSpy|archive-date=14 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201214182703/https://formulaspy.com/f1/verstappen-wins-processional-abu-dhabi-grand-prix-73011|url-status=live}}</ref>

Räikkönen's 323rd Formula One race start at the [[2020 Eifel Grand Prix|Eifel Grand Prix]] saw him break [[Rubens Barrichello]]'s record for most Formula One race starts in history.<ref>{{cite web|title=Raikkonen typically nonchalant about prospect of breaking F1 race starts record at Eifel GP|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.raikkonen-typically-nonchalant-about-prospect-of-breaking-f1-race-starts.5PkdG3Vsc2UOm2SjHouNqT.html|access-date=2 January 2021|website=Formula1.com|archive-date=3 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201203170745/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.raikkonen-typically-nonchalant-about-prospect-of-breaking-f1-race-starts.5PkdG3Vsc2UOm2SjHouNqT.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He won the [[FIA Prize Giving Ceremony#Action of the Year|FIA Action of the Year]] award for his opening lap at the [[2020 Portuguese Grand Prix|Portuguese Grand Prix]], where he overtook 10 cars.<ref>{{cite web|title=Kimi's Portugal launch claims FIA Action of the Year award|url=https://www.sauber-group.com/motorsport/f1-news/kimis-portugal-launch-claims-fia-action-of-the-year-award/|access-date=2 January 2021|website=Sauber Group|archive-date=20 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210120092847/https://www.sauber-group.com/motorsport/f1-news/kimis-portugal-launch-claims-fia-action-of-the-year-award/|url-status=live}}</ref>

web|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.breaking-alfa-romeo-to-retain-raikkonen-and-giovinazzi-in-unchanged-2021.19pDOGCQr6fEivcPLGv4MZ.html|title=Alfa Romeo to retain Raikkonen and points streak is over |url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.azerbaijan-gp-facts-and-stats-hamiltons-record-54-race-points-streak-finally.4uC7XvAGxT3ViRkqxqwpQy.html |website=Formula1.com ==Other racing==
===Rallying===
Räikkönen made his initial rally debut at the [[Arctic Lapland Rally]], which ran from 23 to 24 January 2009, driving a [[Tommi Mäkinen Racing]]-prepared Abarth Grande Punto S2000. He finished in 13th place.<ref>{{cite web | url=http://www.formula1.com/news/headlines/2009/1/8819.html | title=Raikkonen and Hakkinen to contest Arctic Lapland Rally | access-date=22 January 2009 | archive-date=16 January 2009 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090116080135/http://formula1.com/news/headlines/2009/1/8819.html | url-status=live }}</ref> Räikkönen made his WRC debut in the [[2009 Rally Finland]], which took place between 30 July and 2 August, starting just four days after his second-place finish in the [[2009 Hungarian Grand Prix|Hungarian Grand Prix]].<ref name="BBC-29Jun">[https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/motorsport/formula_one/8125463.stm Raikkonen to make rally debut], BBC News, 29 June 2009 21:52 GMT (. Retrieved 30 June 2009)</ref> He was running third in group N and 15th overall before crashing out in [[Väärinmaja]], last stage of Saturday.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/77494 |title=Raikkonen rolls out of rally |work=Autosport |publisher=[[Haymarket Group|Haymarket Publishing]] |date=1 August 2009 |access-date=2 August 2009 |archive-date=4 August 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090804022259/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/77494 |url-status=live }}</ref>

[[File:Kimi Räikkönen - 2010 Rally Bulgaria.jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen driving a Citroën C4 WRC at the [[2010 Rally Bulgaria]]]]
On 4 December 2009, it was announced that Räikkönen would altogether shift from Formula 1 to the [[World Rally Championship]] for the [[2010 World Rally Championship season|2010 season]] as a full-time driver for the [[Citroën Junior Team]],<ref>[http://www.ausmotive.com/2009/12/04/kimi-raikkonen-signs-with-citroen-junior-rally-team.html Kimi Raikkonen signs with Citröen junior rally team] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100210185237/http://www.ausmotive.com/2009/12/04/kimi-raikkonen-signs-with-citroen-junior-rally-team.html |date=10 February 2010 }} AUSmotive.com, 4 December 2009</ref> and that he would be driving a [[Red Bull]]-sponsored [[Citroën C4 WRC]] with his co-driver, Kaj Lindström. As members of the team, the pair were scheduled to participate in 12 of 13 rallies in the 2010 WRC calendar, the exception being Rally New Zealand.

On 3 April 2010, Räikkönen scored his first WRC points when he finished eighth in the [[2010 Jordan Rally|Jordan Rally]]. Consequently, he became the second driver after [[Carlos Reutemann]] to score championship points in both Formula One and the World Rally Championship.<ref>{{cite web | title=Räikkönen teki suomalaista moottoriurheiluhistoriaa! | work=[[MTV3]] | url=http://www.mtv3.fi/urheilu/ralli/uutiset.shtml/arkistot/ralli/2010/04/1092971 | date=3 April 2010 | access-date=3 April 2010 | language=fi-FI | archive-date=6 April 2010 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100406163856/http://www.mtv3.fi/urheilu/ralli/uutiset.shtml/arkistot/ralli/2010/04/1092971 | url-status=live }}</ref> In the next WRC event, the [[2010 Rally of Turkey|Rally of Turkey]], Räikkönen improved his best result with a fifth-place finish, 6m 44.3s off the winner, [[Sébastien Loeb]]. This result saw him beat established and more experienced drivers in the field.

[[File:Rally Finland 2010 - EK 1 - Kimi Räikkönen.jpg|thumb|Räikkönen at the [[2010 Rally Finland]]]]
In the [[2010 Rally Finland]], the retired four-time World Rally Champion [[Juha Kankkunen]] entered the race and said that if Räikkönen cannot beat him then he might as well go back to Formula One. The two were in a close battle for seventh until Räikkönen had a crash on the 12th stage of the rally.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ewrc-results.com/results/580-neste-oil-rally-finland-2010/?s=11712|title=Stage results Neste Oil Rally Finland 2010|last=eWRC-results.com|website=eWRC-results.com|access-date=18 August 2019|archive-date=7 May 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190507170742/https://www.ewrc-results.com/results/580-neste-oil-rally-finland-2010/?s=11712|url-status=live}}</ref> He finished seventh in the [[2010 Rallye Deutschland|Rallye Deutschland]], his second ever asphalt rally, while scoring his first ever career stage win on the last stage of the rally.

On 18 September 2010, Räikkönen achieved his first rally win when he participated in the Rallye Vosgien 2010 in France. He won all six stages in the asphalt rally.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Evans |first1=David |title=Raikkonen wins French national rally |url=http://classic.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/86817 |website=Autosport.com |access-date=29 November 2019 |date=19 September 2010 }}{{Dead link|date=June 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> Räikkönen could not start in the [[2010 Rally Catalunya|Rally Catalunya]] because he crashed during the shakedown, leaving the roll cage damaged, and the team did not have enough time to repair it. Subsequently, Räikkönen decided to not take part in the rally at all, even when he could by super rally rules. The reason was stated to be saving the car.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.autoevolution.com/news/raikkonen-crashes-out-of-catalunya-during-shakedown-25482.html |title=Raikkonen Crashes Out of Catalunya during Shakedown |date=21 October 2010 |publisher=autoevolution |access-date=23 December 2010 |archive-date=10 June 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120610153604/http://www.autoevolution.com/news/raikkonen-crashes-out-of-catalunya-during-shakedown-25482.html |url-status=live }}</ref> He would finish eighth in the [[2010 Wales Rally GB|Wales Rally GB]], the final rally of the season. He scored 25 points during the season to finish 10th overall in the championship, the best result for a rookie that year.

[[File:2011 Rallye Deutschland - Kimi Räikkönen 5.jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen at the [[2011 Rallye Deutschland]]]]
Räikkönen entered the [[2011 World Rally Championship season]] under his own team, [[ICE 1 Racing]]. He drove a [[Citroën DS3 WRC]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.wrc.com/news/huge-entry-for-world-rally-championship-opener/?fid=14024 |title=World Rally Championship – News – Huge entry for World Rally Championship opener |publisher=Wrc.com |access-date=29 March 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120924101537/http://www.wrc.com/news/huge-entry-for-world-rally-championship-opener/?fid=14024 |archive-date=24 September 2012 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He finished eighth in the opening round, [[2011 Rally Sweden|Rally Sweden]]. Skipping the Mexico event, he next competed in [[2011 Rally de Portugal|Rally Portugal]] and finished seventh. He finished sixth in the following [[2011 Jordan Rally|Jordan Rally]], but would skip the next two rounds.

Räikkönen returned in the [[2011 Acropolis Rally|Acropolis Rally]], finishing seventh. He would score points in his home rally, [[2011 Rally Finland|Rally Finland]], after finishing ninth. He equaled his best result of the season with sixth in [[2011 Rallye Deutschland|Rallye Deutschland]], his seventh consecutive points finish and sixth consecutive points finish of the season. After the good form, the season ended in three retirements in the last three rallies. Räikkönen scored 34 points during the season, nine more than in 2010. Despite the increased number of points, he would again finish 10th in the championship.

===NASCAR===
[[File:Kimi raikkonen (52780844274) (cropped).jpg|thumb|Räikkönen in 2023]]
It was reported on 29 March 2011 that Räikkönen would try his hand at [[NASCAR]] starting in the [[NASCAR Truck Series|Truck Series]] in the summer of 2011 with an eye on also running in the [[NASCAR Xfinity Series|Nationwide]] and [[NASCAR Cup Series|Sprint Cup Series]].<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/90331 |title=NASCAR News: Raikkonen to compete in NASCAR |work=Autosport |access-date=29 March 2011 |date=29 March 2011 |archive-date=15 July 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715233434/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/90331 |url-status=live }}</ref> On 2 April Räikkönen signed a deal with Toyota team [[Kyle Busch Motorsports]] to run a limited schedule in the Truck Series.

On 20 May, Räikkönen debuted at the [[North Carolina Education Lottery 200 (Charlotte)|North Carolina Education Lottery 200]] at [[Charlotte Motor Speedway]] with a strong finish of 15th, though he started the day with tough practice sessions and qualified only at 31 out of 37 trucks. His race craft was well received by team crew chief Rick Ren and his teammate [[Kyle Busch]]. Räikkönen went on to race in the Nationwide Series at the same track on 28 May driving for [[Joe Nemechek]] and [[NEMCO Motorsports]]. He finished 27th after having debris stuck on the grille of his car and getting a penalty for speeding in the pitlane.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.usatoday.com/sports/motor/nascar/2011-05-28-Kimi-Raikkonen-27th-in-NASCAR-Nationwide-debut_N.htm |title=Kimi Raikkonen is 27th in challenging Nationwide debut |work=USA Today |date=28 May 2011 |access-date=13 July 2011 |first=Nate |last=Ryan |archive-date=9 August 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110809093058/http://www.usatoday.com/sports/motor/nascar/2011-05-28-Kimi-Raikkonen-27th-in-NASCAR-Nationwide-debut_N.htm |url-status=live }}</ref> Later in the month, Räikkönen tested [[Robby Gordon]]'s car at [[Infineon Raceway]], with plans of Gordon fielding a two-car team for him and Räikkönen at the [[2011 Toyota/Save Mart 350|Toyota/Save Mart 350]]. However, Räikkönen crashed the car in the test, and the deal with [[Robby Gordon Motorsports]] fell through.<ref>{{cite web|last=Pockrass|first=Bob|url=http://www.sportingnews.com/nascar-news/2055174-deal-falls-through-for-kimi-raikkonen-to-drive-for-robby-gordon-at-infineon|title=Deal falls through for Kimi Raikkonen to drive for Robby Gordon at Infineon Raceway|publisher=[[Sporting News]]|date=18 June 2011|access-date=13 May 2016|archive-date=3 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160603183927/http://www.sportingnews.com/nascar-news/2055174-deal-falls-through-for-kimi-raikkonen-to-drive-for-robby-gordon-at-infineon|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[File:Kimi raikkonen (52780055682).jpg|thumb|left|Räikkönen at the [[Circuit of the Americas]] in 2023]]
In May 2022, [[Trackhouse Racing]] announced that Räikkönen would make his Cup Series debut in the No. 91 Chevrolet at the [[2022 Go Bowling at The Glen]] at [[Watkins Glen International]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Medland |first1=Chris |title=Raikkonen to make Cup Series debut for Trackhouse at Watkins Glen |url=https://racer.com/2022/05/26/raikkonen-to-make-cup-series-debut-for-trackhouse-at-watkins-glen/ |website=RACER |access-date=26 May 2022 |date=26 May 2022 |archive-date=26 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220526141747/https://racer.com/2022/05/26/raikkonen-to-make-cup-series-debut-for-trackhouse-at-watkins-glen/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Having previously only driven the car in an acclimatisation test at [[Virginia International Raceway]] and one 20-minute practice session, Räikkönen qualified 27th out of 39 cars, notably ahead of fellow debutant [[Mike Rockenfeller]] and former Formula One driver [[Daniil Kvyat]].<ref name=":2">{{Cite web |date=22 August 2022 |title=Raikkonen's impressive NASCAR Cup debut ends in crash |url=https://the-race.com/nascar/kimi-raikkonens-impressive-nascar-cup-debut-ends-in-crash/ |access-date=16 October 2022 |website=The Race |language=en-GB |archive-date=16 October 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221016173337/https://the-race.com/nascar/kimi-raikkonens-impressive-nascar-cup-debut-ends-in-crash/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=NASCAR: Raikkonen sets 27th fastest time in qualifying – Athletistic |url=https://athletistic.com/formula-1/72774.html |access-date=16 October 2022 |website=athletistic.com |date=20 August 2022 |language=en-US |archive-date=16 October 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221016173338/https://athletistic.com/formula-1/72774.html |url-status=live }}</ref> In the race, he ran in the top ten at one point,<ref name=":2" /> but was classified 37th after being crashed out of 25th place following contact from [[Loris Hezemans]] trying to find a way past a spinning [[Austin Dillon]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nascar.com/news-media/2022/08/21/cup-series-2022-watkins-glen-kimi-raikkonen-crash/ |title=Kimi Räikkönen's Cup Series debut ends early in Watkins Glen crash |website=NASCAR.com |publisher=NASCAR Digital Media, LLC |date=21 August 2022 |access-date=22 August 2022 |archive-date=21 August 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220821235451/https://www.nascar.com/news-media/2022/08/21/cup-series-2022-watkins-glen-kimi-raikkonen-crash/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

In March 2023, Räikkönen returned to the No. 91 at the [[2023 EchoPark Automotive Grand Prix|Circuit of the Americas]], where he qualified 22nd and ran in the mid-pack for most of the race, before a pit stop just before a caution period resulted in him rising to fourth place with nine laps remaining.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Raikkonen "got unlucky" with COTA chaos on NASCAR return |url=https://www.autosport.com/nascar/news/raikkonen-got-unlucky-with-cota-chaos-on-nascar-return/10449073/ |access-date=29 March 2023 |website=www.autosport.com |date=27 March 2023 |language=en |archive-date=29 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230329155239/https://www.autosport.com/nascar/news/raikkonen-got-unlucky-with-cota-chaos-on-nascar-return/10449073/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A chaotic end to the race with multiple restarts and incidents saw him ultimately finish 29th.<ref>{{Cite web |date=26 March 2023 |title=What happened in Raikkonen and Button's Austin NASCAR outing |url=https://the-race.com/nascar/what-happened-in-raikkonen-and-buttons-austin-nascar-outing/ |access-date=29 March 2023 |website=The Race |language=en-GB |archive-date=29 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230329155240/https://the-race.com/nascar/what-happened-in-raikkonen-and-buttons-austin-nascar-outing/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
