|last=Kanerva |first=Juha |date=20 October 2023 |website=[[Helsingin Sanomat]] |language=fi |access-date=1 November 2023 |archive-date=1 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231101025206/https://www.hs.fi/urheilu/art-2000009936912.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Former Ferrari employee Gino Rosato is known as a good friend of Räikkönen and the godfather of his son Robin.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://scuderiafans.com/ferraris-gino-rosato-sees-a-bring-robin-raikkonen-f1-here-we-come/|title=Ferrari's Gino Rosato sees a bright future for Robin Raikkonen: “F1, here we come!|website=Scuderiafans.com|access-date=24 December 2025}}</ref>

Räikkönen's hobbies include [[snowboarding]] and [[ice hockey]].<ref>{{cite web | author=Marx, Jens | title=Cool Raikkonen becomes a Tifosi favourite | work=[[Bangkok Post]] | url=http://www.bangkokpost.com/sportsplus/sportsplus.php?id=122854 | access-date=23 October 2007}}</ref> He has also competed in several different kinds of motorsport events. In March 2007, while his Formula One rivals were in Australia preparing for the season opener, Räikkönen competed in a [[snowmobile]] race in Finland under the pseudonym "[[James Hunt]]", referring to the 1976 world champion whose "playboy" lifestyle has been compared with Räikkönen's own.<ref>{{cite news | author=Benson, Andrew | title=Raikkonen the playboy king | work=BBC | url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/motorsport/formula_one/7055633.stm | access-date=23 October 2007 | date=21 October 2007 | archive-date=23 October 2007 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071023063121/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/motorsport/formula_one/7055633.stm | url-status=live }}</ref> Räikkönen won the Enduro Sprint race by over 20 seconds with his [[Lynx (snowmobile)|Lynx]].<ref>{{cite news | title=Raikkonen wins in the snow | work=[[Autosport]] | url=http://www.autosport.com/news/grapevine.php/id/57244 | access-date=23 October 2007 | date=12 March 2007 | archive-date=16 September 2007 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070916164237/http://www.autosport.com/news/grapevine.php/id/57244 | url-status=live }}</ref> Later in the year, he and two friends entered a powerboat race in the Finnish harbour city of [[Hanko, Finland|Hanko]] while wearing [[gorilla suit]]s. Again, he raced under the name "James Hunt".<ref>{{cite news | author=Baker, Andrew | title=Kimi Raikkonen has the last word |work=The Daily Telegraph | url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/columnists/andrewbaker/2323856/Kimi-Raikkonen-has-the-last-word.html | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091001181241/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/columnists/andrewbaker/2323856/Kimi-Raikkonen-has-the-last-word.html | url-status=dead | archive-date=1 October 2009 | access-date=5 November 2012}}</ref> They then won a prize for the best-dressed crew.<ref>{{cite news | author=Baldwin, Alan | title=Raikkonen questioned about day out as a gorilla | work=[[Reuters]] | url=https://www.reuters.com/article/sportsNews/idUSHAR26992720070802 | access-date=25 December 2007 | date=2 August 2007 | archive-date=15 April 2008 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080415045129/http://www.reuters.com/article/sportsNews/idUSHAR26992720070802 | url-status=live }}</ref>

In August 2008, it was announced that Räikkönen would appear on a set of Finnish postage stamps. The stamps, which were released to commemorate the [[Posti Group|Finnish postal service's]] 370th anniversary, feature images of him racing and on the podium, with the words "F1 World Champion '07 Kimi Räikkönen".<ref>{{cite news|title=Kimi gets Finnish stamp of approval |publisher=ITV-F1 |url=http://www.itv-f1.com/news_article.aspx?id=43630 |date=14 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080818062702/http://www.itv-f1.com/news_article.aspx?id=43630 |archive-date=18 August 2008}}</ref> ''[[Forbes]]'' magazine listed Räikkönen 36th in their 2008 "Celebrity 100" as the 26th highest paid celebrity and fifth highest paid sportsman.<ref name=forbes_raikkonen>{{cite news|url=https://www.forbes.com/lists/2008/53/celebrities08_Kimi-Raikkonen_53XE.html|title=Forbes 100 Celebrities 2008 – No. 36 Kimi Raikkonen|date=11 June 2008|access-date=2 September 2017|archive-date=7 November 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107033132/https://www.forbes.com/lists/2008/53/celebrities08_Kimi-Raikkonen_53XE.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The same list in 2009 recorded him as the second highest-paid athlete.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.forbes.com/2009/06/17/top-earning-athletes-business-sports-top-earning-athletes.html |title=The World's Highest-Paid Athletes |work=Forbes |date=17 June 2009 |access-date=13 September 2009 |first=Kurt |last=Badenhausen |archive-date=5 August 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805190839/https://www.forbes.com/2009/06/17/top-earning-athletes-business-sports-top-earning-athletes.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

In 2011, Räikkönen founded his own [[Motocross World Championship]] team, [[Ice 1 Racing]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.ice1racing.fi/ |title=ICE1RACING |publisher=Ice1racing.fi |access-date=5 November 2011 |archive-date=30 October 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111030014149/http://www.ice1racing.fi/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The team consists of MX1 rider Toni Eriksson and MX2 rider Ludde Söderberg and is managed by seven-time enduro world champion [[Kari Tiainen]]. The Ice 1 Racing team also supports six junior riders competing in the Finnish national championship.

In 2018, Ferrari filed legal action on behalf of Räikkönen with Canadian authorities. The complaint claimed Räikkönen was being extorted by a woman demanding compensation and threatening to publicly accuse him of [[sexual misconduct]] at a function after the [[2016 Canadian Grand Prix]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://speedcafe.com/raikkonen-files-extortion-complaint-canada/|title=Raikkonen files extortion complaint in Canada|work=Speedcafe|date=1 June 2018|access-date=1 June 2018|archive-date=22 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210522134245/https://www.speedcafe.com/2018/06/01/raikkonen-files-extortion-complaint-canada/|url-status=live}}</ref>

Räikkönen stated in 2007 that he believes in God. He has claimed his favorite movie to be ''[[Scarface (1983 film)|Scarface]]''.<ref>{{Citation|magazine=F1Racing Magazine|issue=May 2007|author=Evenstar Saima|url=https://kimiraikkonenspace.com/2007/04/25/f1racing-magazine-issue-may-on-kimi/|title=F1Racing Magazine Issue May – on Kimi|date=25 April 2007|access-date=12 December 2021|archive-date=12 December 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211212203205/https://kimiraikkonenspace.com/2007/04/25/f1racing-magazine-issue-may-on-kimi/|url-status=live}}</ref> Räikkönen is also recognized for having a distinctive voice. Räikkönen has said that the reason behind it is a bicycle accident that he suffered when he was 5 years old. Räikkönen fell, hit his neck on the bicycle handlebar, and injured his [[vocal cords]], which never fully healed.<ref>{{cite web | title=Räikkösen puhekyvyn taustalla erikoinen tarina – "poikanne on keskivertoa älykkäämpi" | work=[[Ilta-Sanomat]] | url=https://www.is.fi/formula1/art-2000000531823.html | date=17 August 2012 | access-date=15 August 2020 | language=fi-FI | archive-date=2 September 2021 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210902130816/https://www.is.fi/formula1/art-2000000531823.html | url-status=live }}</ref> Sebastian Vettel and Antonio Giovinazzi, his teammates from Ferrari and Alfa Romeo, are two of his close friends.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kimi hurmasi Vettelin – yksi piirre ylitse muiden |url=https://www.iltalehti.fi/formulat/a/2012041615456409 |access-date=20 January 2023 |website=www.iltalehti.fi |language=fi |archive-date=20 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230120104720/https://www.iltalehti.fi/formulat/a/2012041615456409 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bhattacharjee |first=Somin |date=31 January 2022 |title="It's been a while since we played badminton against each other": Kimi Raikkonen reveals the drivers he plans to stay in touch with during his retirement |url=https://thesportsrush.com/f1-news-its-been-a-while-since-we-played-badminton-against-each-other-kimi-raikkonen-reveals-the-drivers-he-plans-to-stay-in-touch-with-during-his-retirement/ |access-date=20 January 2023 |website=The SportsRush |language=en |archive-date=20 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230120104722/https://thesportsrush.com/f1-news-its-been-a-while-since-we-played-badminton-against-each-other-kimi-raikkonen-reveals-the-drivers-he-plans-to-stay-in-touch-with-during-his-retirement/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Fans |first=Scuderia |date=20 March 2020 |title=Kimi on making friends among F1 drivers: "Seb and Antonio are two real friends. ==Public persona and reaction==
Räikkönen is known for his dislike of giving media interviews, and frequently answers questions in a blunt and monosyllabic manner. In the build-up to the [[2006 Brazilian Grand Prix]], when then-[[ITV (TV network)|ITV]] pundit [[Martin Brundle]] asked Räikkönen why he missed a ceremony in which footballer [[Pelé]] presented Michael Schumacher with a lifetime achievement award, Räikkönen replied "I was having a shit".<ref>[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/oct/21/motorsports.news Motor racing: No small talk, just big deeds from throwback Kimi | Sport | The Observer] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170118072552/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/oct/21/motorsports.news |date=18 January 2017 }}. Guardian (21 October 2007). Retrieved on 16 August 2013.</ref>

During his early years at McLaren, Ron Dennis gave him the nickname, "[[The Iceman (nickname)|Iceman]]", with several layers of meaning; apart from its association with the cold climate of Finland, he is widely considered to have a cool temperament under pressure and also an 'icy' persona with most other drivers, team members and the media. He has said that he is "not here to try to please people. I'm here to do my best"<ref name="Lindberg">{{cite journal|last1=Lindberg|first1=Austin|title=Kimi Räikkönen Is the Most Interesting Man in Formula 1|journal=Car and Driver|date=August 2013|url=http://www.caranddriver.com/features/kimi-raikkonen-is-the-most-interesting-man-in-f1-news-im-here-to-do-my-best-page-2|access-date=23 November 2014|archive-date=16 February 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150216224015/http://www.caranddriver.com/features/kimi-raikkonen-is-the-most-interesting-man-in-f1-news-im-here-to-do-my-best-page-2|url-status=live}}</ref> and, in a post-race interview after winning the [[2012 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix]], "Last time you guys was giving me shit because I didn't really smile enough".<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-11-09 |title=F1 drivers told to mind their tongue |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india/f1-drivers-told-to-mind-their-tongue/story-Gqrx9nVfV7JfvmcqXB3CRO.html |access-date=2025-10-26 |website=[[Hindustan Times]] |language=en}}</ref>

Apart from his on-track driving, off-track instances demonstrating this calm demeanour include being asleep 30 minutes before his first Formula One race<ref name="Maurice Hamilton" /> and eating an ice cream during the temporarily suspended [[2009 Malaysian Grand Prix]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Raikkonen reveals why he ate ice cream in Malaysia 2009|url=http://f1pulse.com/news/2013021535/raikkonen-reveals-why-he-ate-ice-cream-in-malaysia-2009|access-date=23 November 2014|publisher=F1 Pulse|date=15 February 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150703024353/http://f1pulse.com/news/2013021535/raikkonen-reveals-why-he-ate-ice-cream-in-malaysia-2009|archive-date=3 July 2015}}</ref>{{quote box
| quote = I don't think you can have an argument or a problem with Kimi. If you do, the problem is not him, the problem is you.
| source = —[[Sebastian Vettel]], speaking about Räikkönen in 2021.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.gpfans.com/en/f1-news/72780/vettel-to-miss-raikkonen-silence-after-f1-swansong/ |title=Vettel to miss Raikkonen "silence" after F1 swansong |last= |first= |date= 30 November 2021|website=GP Fans |publisher= |access-date= |quote= |archive-date=30 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211130223649/https://www.gpfans.com/en/f1-news/72780/vettel-to-miss-raikkonen-silence-after-f1-swansong/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align = right
| width = 27%
}}

Known to be frustrated by anything that prevents him from simply racing, he is relatively outspoken in his criticism of politics and off-circuit drama in sport. In a rare feature interview in the middle of the 2013 season, Räikkönen said "sometimes in Formula 1 there is politics, and the shit there is stupid".<ref name="Lindberg"/> In the same article, Lotus team principal [[Éric Boullier]] described Räikkönen as someone "doing pretty much whatever he wants".

During the [[2006 Monaco Grand Prix]], his [[McLaren MP4-21]] broke down as the [[heat shield]] had exploded and he was forced to retire; he went from his car straight to his nearby yacht, fully dressed in his racing suit and immediately relaxed in his [[Hot tub|jacuzzi]].<ref>{{cite web |title=6 classic Kimi Raikkonen moments that perfectly sum up F1's coolest driver |url=https://drivetribe.com/p/6-classic-kimi-raikkonen-moments-VsOBTrjrTcGpwAItDHNvtg?iid=T5oKRsPwQgmOmTv6uw50Gg |website=DriveTribe |access-date=14 November 2021 |date=2017 |archive-date=14 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114181426/https://drivetribe.com/p/6-classic-kimi-raikkonen-moments-VsOBTrjrTcGpwAItDHNvtg?iid=T5oKRsPwQgmOmTv6uw50Gg |url-status=live }}</ref>{{quote box
| quote = I don't want to put some kind of limits on how you remember. I mean, I don't care much because I luckily been able to do most of the things how I wish to do it. And whatever they remember, good way or bad way, it's a memory and it's fine for me.
| source = – When asked how he wanted to be remembered by F1 fans from around the world, during his last "Beyond The Grid" appearance.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mui97tlrs1s |title=Farewell To A Legend: Kimi Raikkonen Reflects On His Memorable Career | Beyond The Grid F1 Podcast |publisher=YouTube |date=2021-12-08 |access-date=2022-01-05 |archive-date=10 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211210190405/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mui97tlrs1s |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align = right
| width = 30%
}}

Räikkönen's terse radio communications have sometimes attracted comment. On the 20th lap of the [[2012 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix]], Räikkönen had just taken the lead from Lewis Hamilton and his race engineer [[Simon Rennie]] advised that Fernando Alonso was five seconds behind and that he would be kept informed of Alonso's pace; Räikkönen replied, "Just leave me alone, I know what to do!".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/568665409957112/posts/1208249795998667/|title=F1|website=www.facebook.com|access-date=24 November 2018|archive-date=14 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230414023645/https://www.facebook.com/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2F568665409957112%2Fposts%2F1208249795998667%2F|url-status=live}}</ref> Later in the same race, he admonished his team again, when being told to manage the tyre temperature: "Yes, yes, yes, yes, I'm doing that all the time. You don't have to remind me every ten seconds!".<ref>{{Cite news|title = Raikkonen gives out Lotus T-shirt|work = BBC Sport|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/formula1/20267305|access-date = 5 November 2015|archive-date = 17 October 2015|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20151017131240/http://www.bbc.com/sport/0/formula1/20267305|url-status = live}}</ref> The first quote attracted significant press coverage, and Räikkönen made no attempt to apologise in the post-race interview. It was earlier reported that he printed 500 T-shirts with the quote for the entire Lotus team, but this was later denied by Räikkönen in an interview with [[Formula One Group]] media personnel.<ref name="Grill">{{cite web|title=Grill|url=http://www.formula1.com/content/fom-website/en/video/2016/6/Grill_the_grid_-_Kimi_Raikkonen.html|website=Formula One Official Website|access-date=22 June 2016|archive-date=24 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160624185653/http://www.formula1.com/content/fom-website/en/video/2016/6/Grill_the_grid_-_Kimi_Raikkonen.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The quote was featured on his official website.<ref name="Profile">{{cite web|title=Profile|url=http://www.kimiraikkonen.com/profile.asp|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141007095018/http://www.kimiraikkonen.com/profile.asp|website=Kimi Räikkönen Official Website|archive-date=7 October 2014|url-status=usurped|access-date=23 November 2014}}</ref>

==Helmet==
[[File:Kimi Raikkonen helmet.jpg|thumb|Helmet used when Räikkönen raced for Ferrari seen at [[Museo Ferrari]]]]
[[File:Kimi Räikkönen Logo.svg|thumb|right|upright|The insignia that appears on the top of Räikkönen's helmet]]
Räikkönen's helmet, designed by UffeDesigns, manufactured by [[Arai Helmet|Arai]] (2001–2006, 2012), [[Bell Sports|Bell]] (2013, 2015–2021), and [[Schuberth]] (2007–2009, 2014), slightly changed during the years. His helmet has also always featured a V design running on the circle top (representing a flying bird) and the inscription "Iceman". The trident insignia was painted in white during his time racing for Sauber and McLaren until 2005, and red from 2006 with McLaren and during his time with Ferrari.

Initially his helmet was predominantly blue with white and silver details, but its colours and detailing changed over time. When racing for Ferrari Räikkönen's helmet changed radically: it was white with the middle part black and red with tribal designs. He retained this design in some rallies, although the helmet style was significantly different for this discipline. In WRC and NASCAR he used a blue Stilo helmet with Red Bull's logo, silver and white accents (to resemble Red Bull's can design and Räikkönen's Sauber helmet lines). Upon his return to Formula One, he sported a black helmet with white and red diagonals. For the [[2012 Monaco Grand Prix|Monaco Grand Prix]], he wore a replica of the 1976 [[James Hunt]] helmet. Upon his return to Ferrari in 2014, the base colour became a vibrant red, with white diagonal lines crossing from each side. In 2015, his helmet reverted to a white base. As of 2014, Räikkönen continued to race with a Bell model rather than Ferrari's official supplier Schuberth.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

==Other ventures==
In 2022, after his retirement from Formula One, Räikkönen was named the team principal of the Kawasaki Racing Team for the [[2022 FIM Motocross World Championship|2022 season]] of the [[Motocross World Championship]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Raikkonen becomes Team Principal of Kawasaki motocross squad following F1 retirement |url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.raikkonen-becomes-team-principal-of-kawasaki-motocross-squad-following-f1.2ZZNaVm992rHXWCKAjCENr.html |website=www.formula1.com |access-date=28 January 2022 |date=27 January 2022 |archive-date=2 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220602132218/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.raikkonen-becomes-team-principal-of-kawasaki-motocross-squad-following-f1.2ZZNaVm992rHXWCKAjCENr.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Formula One achievements===
* The [[2007 Chinese Grand Prix]] saw Räikkönen give [[Scuderia Ferrari|Ferrari]] ''as a manufacturer'' their 200th win, as well as their 600th podium.
* The [[2008 French Grand Prix]] saw Räikkönen give Ferrari ''as a manufacturer'' their 200th pole position.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-motor-racing-prix-pole-idUKL2139898520080621|title=Raikkonen secures Ferrari's 200th F1 pole|newspaper=Reuters|date=21 June 2008|access-date=29 April 2017|last1=Baldwin|first1=Alan|archive-date=26 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190826121243/https://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-motor-racing-prix-pole-idUKL2139898520080621|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* The [[2016 Russian Grand Prix]] saw Räikkönen give Ferrari ''as a manufacturer'' their 700th podium.
* Räikkönen is the only driver to win in the V10, V8 and the V6 turbo hybrid engine eras.<ref>{{cite web |last1=George |first1=Dhruv |title=Raikkonen Now Owns a Unique Record |url=https://www.essentiallysports.com/raikkonen-now-owns-unique-record/ |website=Essentially Sports |access-date=31 October 2018 |date=23 October 2018 |archive-date=1 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181101015627/https://www.essentiallysports.com/raikkonen-now-owns-unique-record/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
* Räikkönen is the second driver to win the World Championship after being third in the drivers standings before the final race.
* Räikkönen is the third Ferrari driver to win the World Championship in their first year with the team, after [[Juan Manuel Fangio]] and [[Jody Scheckter]].
* Räikkönen is the third Finnish driver to win the World Championship, after [[Keke Rosberg]] and [[Mika Häkkinen]].
* Räikkönen is the only driver to win a Grand Prix for [[Lotus F1]].
* {{As of|2025}}, Räikkönen is the latest Ferrari driver to win the World Championship.
<!--* He set the fastest lap in F1 history in qualifying at the [[2018 Italian Grand Prix]] with an average speed of {{convert|263.588|km/h|mph}},<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/formula1/45172710|title=Raikkonen takes Italian GP pole with fastest lap in F1 history – radio & text|date=23 August 2018|work=BBC Sport|access-date=1 September 2018|archive-date=31 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180831091131/https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/formula1/45172710|url-status=live}}</ref> eclipsing [[Juan Pablo Montoya]]'s previous record.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.move-over-montoya-raikkonen-breaks-record-for-fastest-lap-in-f1-history.1OyGclYbnOYIuKKEMwyQk8.html|title=Move Over Montoya: Raikkonen breaks record for fastest lap in F1 history|date=1 September 2018|work=Formula 1|access-date=8 September 2020|archive-date=29 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181029152433/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.move-over-montoya-raikkonen-breaks-record-for-fastest-lap-in-f1-history.1OyGclYbnOYIuKKEMwyQk8.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The record was later surpassed by Lewis Hamilton in qualifying for the [[2020 Italian Grand Prix]], with Hamilton setting an average speed of {{convert|264.362|km/h|mph}}.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.fastest-ever-f1-lap-felt-awesome-says-hamilton-as-he-claims-7th-monza-pole.QHVMGh0r0HviWxi63GreA.html|title=Fastest ever F1 lap felt 'awesome' says Hamilton as he claims 7th Monza pole|date=5 September 2020|work=Formula 1|access-date=8 September 2020|archive-date=6 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200906192432/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article.fastest-ever-f1-lap-felt-awesome-says-hamilton-as-he-claims-7th-monza-pole.QHVMGh0r0HviWxi63GreA.html|url-status=live}}</ref>-->
